BRA|NOR.text: Brazil face Norway in the World Cup round of 16, with the Norwegians appearing at the tournament for the first time in 28 years and seeking a maiden quarter-final berth. The tie features a subplot from the Premier League, as Manchester City's Erling Haaland and Arsenal defender Gabriel extend their club rivalry to the international stage.

sources.headline: World Cup Daily live: Mexico fans try to give England sleepless night
Brazil vs. Norway: Gabriel and Haaland take rivalry to international stage
Stay humble - the Premier League feud about to hit world stage

ENG|MEX.text: Jarell Quansah is set to start for England, with Thomas Tuchel and Jordan Henderson having dismissed Mexican media claims about players using Viagra to combat the altitude. Mexican fans gathered outside England's hotel through the early hours, blasting horns and setting off fireworks in an attempt to disrupt the squad's sleep. A shelter-in-place order was issued at the Azteca before kickoff due to severe weather.

sources.headline: World Cup Daily live: Mexico fans try to give England sleepless night; Brazil face Norway
Jarell Quansah to start for England vs. Mexico at World Cup - sources
Mexico fans' serenata outside England hotel woke me up, and probably Three Lions too

BEL|USA.text: The United States meet Belgium in the World Cup round of 16 on Monday. Forward Folarin Balogun remains cleared to play after FIFA rejected the Belgium federation's request for an explanation of his suspended red card, ruling the challenge inadmissible.

sources.headline: World Cup Daily live: Spain knock out Portugal; Kane loses voice; USMNT prepares for Belgium
FIFA reject Belgium request; Balogun still clear to play vs. Belgium
2026 World Cup: How to bet on U.S. vs. Belgium

ESP|POR.text: Spain enter this knockout tie without conceding a goal at the tournament, closing in on a defensive record. The model favours Spain to advance at 61% against Portugal's 39%.

sources.headline: World Cup Daily live: Harry Kane loses voice in hilarious interview, USMNT prepares for Belgium
The numbers behind Spain defence chasing World Cup history

ARG|EGY.text: Argentina face Egypt in the World Cup round of 16 in Atlanta, with Lionel Messi named in the starting lineup. Coach Lionel Scaloni said his side "will give everything" in the tie.

sources.headline: World Cup Daily live: Messi starts as Argentina prepare to face Egypt
Egypt and Argentina fans descend on Atlanta for round-of-16 clash
Scaloni: Argentina 'will give everything' in Egypt clash

COL|SUI.text: Colombia coach Lorenzo has stressed the need for "tactical discipline" against Switzerland in this knockout tie. The model favours Colombia to advance, 62% to 38%.

sources.headline: Lorenzo: Colombia wil need 'tactical discipline' vs. Switzerland
Qarabag's Duran poised for Celtic medical

FRA|MAR.text: France meet Morocco in the quarterfinals, with Morocco missing forward Ismael Saibari. Brahim Diaz features in the Moroccan squad as they aim to compete with a France side built around Kylian Mbappe's attack.

sources.headline: World Cup Daily live: Mexico star Mora's high school graduation, France-Morocco kicks off quarterfinals
How do Morocco stop France's unparalleled attack?
Morocco press conference chaos leaves Brahim Diaz forgetting the question

BEL|ESP.text: Spain meet Belgium in the World Cup quarterfinal at SoFi Stadium in Los Angeles on Friday. Coach Luis de la Fuente has highlighted an "improved" Lamine Yamal ahead of the tie, while Belgium goalkeeper Thibaut Courtois faces Spain in an official match for the first time in his career.

sources.headline: U.S. star Christian Pulisic suffered microfracture vs. Belgium
Spain's Luis de la Fuente excited to see Lamine Yamal 2.0 vs. Belgium
Courtois: Spain are one of the favourites to win the World Cup
